context: Tests whether the agent designs interfaces for testability: accepting external dependencies as parameters (not creating them internally), returning results rather than relying on side effects, and keeping the public surface area small.

checklist.name: Dependency injection
checklist.description: EmailService accepts the external email provider client as a constructor argument or function parameter — does NOT instantiate it internally (no 'new SendGridClient()' or 'new MailgunClient()' inside the implementation)

checklist.name: Dependencies not created internally
checklist.description: No external service clients, HTTP clients, or SDK instances are created inside EmailService methods — all external dependencies come in from outside

checklist.name: Returns results
checklist.description: EmailService methods return a value (e.g. a result object, success boolean, or message ID) rather than only producing side effects with void/undefined returns

checklist.name: Small public surface
checklist.description: EmailService exposes 3 or fewer public methods (e.g. sendWelcome, sendPasswordReset, send) — not a large number of methods

checklist.name: Simple parameters
checklist.description: Public methods take simple, minimal parameters — not large config objects or many positional arguments (simple params = fewer things to set up in tests)

checklist.name: Tests use mock provider
checklist.description: Example tests pass a mock/stub email provider client into EmailService — they do not make real network calls

checklist.name: Interface design explained
checklist.description: interface-design.md explains at least 2 interface decisions made for testability and the alternatives considered

checklist.name: No internal collaborator creation
checklist.description: Implementation does not create loggers, config loaders, or other internal collaborators inside methods — these are either passed in or not present

checklist.name: Complexity hidden
checklist.description: Complex details (email template rendering, provider-specific formatting) are hidden inside the implementation, not exposed in the public interface

checklist.name: Fewer methods = fewer tests
checklist.description: interface-design.md mentions that fewer public methods reduces the number of tests needed (acknowledging surface-area as a testability concern)
